run_metadata.notes: Threat model: Lyra is an audit orchestration dashboard over projects, findings, repair queues, and Linear sync; data includes security audit content and repo metadata. Users are implied single-tenant (solo dev) but deployment is often a public URL. Highest-value targets are aggregated findings (competitive/security intel), orchestration state, and abuse of server-held API keys (Linear). Likely vectors: unauthenticated REST calls to Next.js API routes, and queue/sync abuse if the host is reachable.

agent.name: security-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Practical security and privacy risk assessment for auth, secrets, validation, and data exposure.

files_skipped: worker/src/** — worker not fully traced for auth boundaries; batch_fix.py / root Python CLIs out of dashboard API scope
coverage.incomplete_reason: Worker process, hosting-specific hardening, and all client components for secret handling not exhaustively reviewed; CORS assumed Next defaults.

findings.title: Dashboard APIs allow unauthenticated read/write of projects and findings
findings.description: Project and mutation routes call getRepository() with no session, API key, or middleware. Attack: anyone who can reach the deployed origin issues GET /api/projects to list every project and findings, PUT/DELETE to destroy or overwrite data, POST /api/import or POST /api/sync/audit to inject or merge findings, and POST/DELETE /api/engine/queue to manipulate the repair queue. No per-user isolation. Realistic when the dashboard is hosted on Netlify/Vercel without network restrictions.

proof_hooks.summary: GET/POST /api/projects have no auth before repo.list/create
PUT/DELETE project by name with no authorization
POST /api/import creates/updates projects from arbitrary JSON
POST /api/sync/audit merges filesystem findings into DB without auth

steps: Deploy or run dashboard with DATABASE_URL set.
Observe 200 JSON with projects and findings without Authorization.

repro_steps: Run dashboard with DB containing projects.
Confirm full project and finding payload without credentials.
findings.impact: Full confidentiality and integrity loss for audit data; possible destruction of audit history.

suggested_fix.approach: Require Authorization: Bearer <ORCHESTRATION_ENQUEUE_SECRET> or DASHBOARD_API_SECRET on all /api/* except explicit health; or Netlify/Vercel auth + Next middleware. For multi-user, Supabase Auth + RLS on project rows.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Breaking for unauthenticated CI; document header for cron/automation.

findings.title: GET /api/orchestration/jobs exposes job and run history without authentication
findings.description: POST is protected when ORCHESTRATION_ENQUEUE_SECRET is set, but GET returns recent jobs and runs to any caller. Attack: reconnaissance on audit schedules and project names.
findings.impact: Operational metadata leak; aids targeted abuse.

suggested_fix.approach: Apply same Bearer/header check as POST for GET.
tests_needed: GET without secret → 401 when secret configured

findings.title: POST /api/sync/linear/push callable without auth; burns LINEAR_API_KEY
findings.description: Any client can POST with projectName to create/update Linear issues using the server LINEAR_API_KEY. Attack: spam Linear, exhaust rate limits, expose finding titles in a shared workspace.

proof_hooks.summary: POST validates Linear config and body only, not caller

findings.impact: Abuse of third-party API; unsolicited issues.

suggested_fix.approach: Require same API secret as other mutations before calling Linear APIs.

findings.title: GET /api/durable-state returns orchestration config and events without auth
findings.description: Exposes durable state configuration and recent events to any caller.

suggested_fix.approach: Gate with shared secret or session.

findings.title: npm audit: Next.js moderate advisory (image cache disk growth)
findings.description: GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8: unbounded next/image cache. Risk mainly disk exhaustion on persistent Node hosts using next/image heavily.

proof_hooks.summary: npm audit reports 1 moderate next issue

findings.impact: Potential DoS via disk fill on long-running image-heavy deployments.

suggested_fix.approach: Upgrade Next to patched release per advisory when feasible.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Major version bump may need app changes.

findings.title: API routes return raw Error.message to clients on 500
findings.description: Handlers often return e.message in JSON. May leak DB or path hints if errors are triggered.

suggested_fix.approach: Generic client message in production; log server-side.

next_actions.action: Add global API authentication on /api/* for any internet-facing deploy.
next_actions.rationale: Blocker — full exposure of security audit data.
